amdp-hana-001.interviewScriptHinglish: Sir, AMDP — ABAP Managed Database Procedures — ek mechanism hai jo ABAP code ko directly SAP HANA database pe execute karne deta hai. Yeh ek ABAP class ka method hota hai jiska body SQLScript mein likha hota hai, aur execution application server par nahi balki HANA database pe hoti hai. Class ko IF_AMDP_MARKER_HDB interface implement karni padti hai, aur method declaration mein BY DATABASE PROCEDURE FOR HDB LANGUAGE SQLSCRIPT likhna padta hai. Practically, mere project mein sales aggregation report ke liye AMDP use kiya tha — 5 crore rows ka GROUP BY application server pe 4 minutes le raha tha, AMDP se HANA pushdown ke baad 8 seconds mein complete hua. Iska benefit yeh hai ki data move nahi hota, sirf final result aata hai — network aur memory dono efficiently use hoti hain.
amdp-hana-001.interviewScriptEnglish: AMDP, or ABAP Managed Database Procedures, is a mechanism that allows ABAP class methods to execute directly on the SAP HANA database as stored procedures written in SQLScript. The class must implement the IF_AMDP_MARKER_HDB marker interface, and the method is declared with BY DATABASE PROCEDURE FOR HDB LANGUAGE SQLSCRIPT. We use AMDP for code pushdown scenarios such as complex aggregations, multi-step transformations, and analytical reports where processing millions of rows on the application server would be inefficient. In my project, we used AMDP for a finance summary report and reduced runtime from four minutes to eight seconds. The key business value is faster reports, reduced network traffic, and better utilization of HANA's in-memory columnar engine.

wordByWordSamjho.meaning: Marker interface signalling that the class contains HANA AMDP methods
Method addition declaring the body as a database procedure, not ABAP
Specifies the database is SAP HANA (HDB)
States the body language is HANA SQLScript
Restricts the AMDP body to read-only operations, blocks DML
Lists database tables and views referenced inside the SQLScript body
Exception class raised when an AMDP fails at runtime
Pass-by-value exporting parameter that returns the tabular result to ABAP
SQL clause that aggregates rows sharing common column values
Aggregate function that totals a numeric column at the database layer

commonMistakesSection.mistake: Forgetting to list a table in the USING clause
commonMistakesSection.whyWrong: SAP transport and dependency tracking fail; activation error or unexpected runtime behaviour
commonMistakesSection.correctApproach: List every transparent table, CDS view, or other AMDP referenced inside the body explicitly in USING

commonMistakesSection.mistake: Writing ABAP statements inside the AMDP body
commonMistakesSection.whyWrong: AMDP body is pure SQLScript that runs on HANA — ABAP statements like LOOP, AUTHORITY-CHECK, MESSAGE fail to activate
commonMistakesSection.correctApproach: Keep ABAP logic in the calling wrapper method; use only SQLScript constructs inside the AMDP body

commonMistakesSection.mistake: Assuming the standard ABAP debugger can step into an AMDP
commonMistakesSection.whyWrong: AMDP runs on HANA, so Eclipse breakpoints do not stop inside the body
commonMistakesSection.correctApproach: Use SAP HANA Database Explorer or HANA Studio; add intermediate tabular variables to inspect step-by-step

commonMistakesSection.mistake: Skipping authorization checks because the AMDP runs on HANA
commonMistakesSection.whyWrong: AMDP cannot run AUTHORITY-CHECK; data leaks if calling ABAP layer also skips checks
commonMistakesSection.correctApproach: Perform AUTHORITY-CHECK in the ABAP wrapper before calling the AMDP method

amdp-hana-001.quickRevisionNotes:
What: ABAP class method whose body is SQLScript executed directly on the SAP HANA database.

Used for:
- Code pushdown of aggregations, joins, and analytical logic
- Complex multi-step transformations on large datasets
- Backing CDS table functions with procedural SQLScript logic

Key syntax / tables / Tcodes:
- INTERFACES if_amdp_marker_hdb in class definition
- METHOD ... BY DATABASE PROCEDURE FOR HDB LANGUAGE SQLSCRIPT OPTIONS READ-ONLY USING <tables>
- ADT in Eclipse for development; HANA Database Explorer for debugging
- Exception class CX_AMDP_ERROR for error handling in calling ABAP

Interview keyword: managed code pushdown via ABAP-owned SQLScript

amdp-hana-002.interviewScriptHinglish: Sir, CDS Views aur AMDP dono HANA pe code pushdown ke tools hain lekin nature alag hai. CDS view declarative DDL hai — tum data model define karte ho with annotations like @OData.publish, @Analytics — Fiori, OData, ABAP sab consume kar sakte hain. AMDP procedural SQLScript hai — multi-step logic, tabular variables, conditional processing. Practically, mere project mein sales region-wise aggregation ke liye CDS view banaya kyunki Fiori app aur OData service dono use karne the. Lekin inventory aging report mein AMDP use kiya kyunki age buckets ka complex date logic ek single CDS SELECT mein express nahi ho sakta tha. Iska benefit yeh hai ki right tool for right job — CDS for reusable data models, AMDP for complex calculations, aur CDS Table Function se dono ko combine bhi kar sakte ho.
amdp-hana-002.interviewScriptEnglish: CDS views are declarative data models written in DDL, ideal for read scenarios that need reusability, annotations, and integration with OData and Fiori. AMDP is procedural SQLScript suited for complex multi-step database logic that cannot be expressed in a single declarative SELECT. In our project we used CDS views for Fiori KPI tiles and OData services because annotations gave us automatic metadata; we used AMDP for inventory aging where conditional bucket assignments and date arithmetic required procedural constructs. The business value is choosing the right pushdown mechanism — CDS for reusable, broadly consumable data layers, and AMDP for heavy calculation logic. When needed, a CDS Table Function backed by an AMDP combines both benefits.

wordByWordSamjho.meaning: CDS DDL keyword that introduces a view definition in ADT
Annotation that maps the CDS entity to a SQL view name in DDIC
Marks the CDS view as CUBE, FACT, or DIMENSION for analytical consumption
Lazy navigational join evaluated only when the consumer accesses the path
AMDP method declaration routing execution to HANA SQLScript
AMDP guardrail preventing INSERT, UPDATE, or DELETE inside the body
HANA SQLScript function returning the day difference between two dates
Set-based conditional used to assign buckets inline in a SELECT
Aggregate function summing the net value column on HANA

commonMistakesSection.mistake: Trying to call an AMDP from inside a CDS view
commonMistakesSection.whyWrong: CDS DDL is declarative and cannot invoke procedural code
commonMistakesSection.correctApproach: Use a CDS Table Function that is implemented by the AMDP method when CDS-side procedural logic is needed

commonMistakesSection.mistake: Using AMDP for every analytical requirement
commonMistakesSection.whyWrong: Simple aggregations are cleaner, more reusable, and easier to maintain as CDS views
commonMistakesSection.correctApproach: Default to CDS views; switch to AMDP only when procedural multi-step logic is unavoidable

commonMistakesSection.mistake: Skipping access control on a CDS view by setting authorizationCheck to NOT_REQUIRED without justification
commonMistakesSection.whyWrong: Bypasses ABAP authorization and creates a security gap visible to every consumer
commonMistakesSection.correctApproach: Keep @AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #CHECK and define a DCL role that restricts rows via pfcg_auth

commonMistakesSection.mistake: Performing DML in AMDP without considering ABAP locking
commonMistakesSection.whyWrong: ENQUEUE/DEQUEUE and change documents do not apply at the HANA layer
commonMistakesSection.correctApproach: Keep AMDPs read-only with OPTIONS READ-ONLY; perform writes through ABAP MODIFY with proper locks

amdp-hana-002.quickRevisionNotes:
What: CDS views are declarative DDL data models, AMDP is procedural SQLScript inside an ABAP class method.

Used for:
- CDS: reusable data layer for Fiori, OData, analytics with annotations
- AMDP: complex multi-step calculations, conditional transformations
- CDS Table Function: bridges both by exposing AMDP via CDS

Key syntax / tables / Tcodes:
- define view ZI_... with @OData.publish, @Analytics, @AccessControl
- METHOD ... BY DATABASE PROCEDURE FOR HDB LANGUAGE SQLSCRIPT USING ...
- define table function ... implemented by method <class>=><method>
- Eclipse ADT for both; HANA Database Explorer for AMDP debug

Interview keyword: declarative CDS vs procedural AMDP, bridged by CDS Table Function

amdp-hana-003.interviewScriptHinglish: Sir, SQLScript SAP HANA ka procedural SQL language hai jo standard SQL ko extend karta hai with scalar variables, tabular variables, IF-THEN-ELSE aur procedural constructs. AMDP method ke andar SQLScript hi likha jata hai. Sabse important feature hai tabular variable — ek intermediate result ko table ki tarah store karte hain, phir colon notation se reference karte hain, jaise FROM :lt_orders. Practically, mere project mein vendor aging report ke liye SQLScript use kiya — pehle lt_invoices mein open items liye, phir DAYS_BETWEEN se age calculate ki, phir CASE WHEN se buckets banaye, sab HANA ke andar set-based. Iska benefit yeh hai ki LOOP avoid karke set operations use karte hain, jisse HANA ka parallel columnar engine fully utilize hota hai aur performance massive improve hoti hai.
amdp-hana-003.interviewScriptEnglish: SQLScript is SAP HANA's procedural extension of SQL, supporting scalar variables, tabular variables, conditional logic, and built-in functions. It is the language used inside AMDP method bodies. Tabular variables hold intermediate result sets in HANA memory and are referenced with a colon prefix, enabling multi-step transformations without sending data to the application server. We use SQLScript for date arithmetic with DAYS_BETWEEN, NULL-safe aggregations with COALESCE, and conditional bucketing with CASE WHEN. The business value is significant performance gains: set-based operations on HANA's columnar engine process millions of rows in seconds, where ABAP loops would take minutes. Avoiding row-by-row LOOP constructs is the key best practice.

wordByWordSamjho.meaning: Declares a scalar SQLScript variable with type and optional default
Colon-prefix syntax used to reference a tabular or scalar variable
Returns only matching rows from both tables on the join condition
Returns all rows from the left side plus matching rows from the right
Aggregates rows that share values in listed columns
Returns the first non-NULL argument, used as a NULL-safe default
HANA built-in returning today's date on the database server
Adds or subtracts years from a date, returning the new date
Aggregate functions executed inside HANA on the entire result set
Sorts the result set ascending or descending

commonMistakesSection.mistake: Forgetting the colon prefix when referencing a tabular or scalar variable
commonMistakesSection.whyWrong: SQLScript parser cannot resolve the identifier and activation fails
commonMistakesSection.correctApproach: Always use :lt_name for tabular and :lv_name for scalar references inside the body

commonMistakesSection.mistake: Writing LOOP constructs to process rows one by one
commonMistakesSection.whyWrong: Bypasses HANA's parallel columnar engine and performs worse than ABAP
commonMistakesSection.correctApproach: Use set-based alternatives like CASE WHEN, GROUP BY, and JOINs across the whole result set

commonMistakesSection.whyWrong: NULL propagates through arithmetic and skews SUM, AVG, or breaks WHERE comparisons
commonMistakesSection.correctApproach: Wrap nullable columns with COALESCE(col, 0) or use IS NULL / IS NOT NULL for checks

commonMistakesSection.mistake: Using INNER JOIN with SERIES_GENERATE_DATE for time-series reports
commonMistakesSection.whyWrong: Periods with no transactions are dropped, leaving gaps in the report
commonMistakesSection.correctApproach: LEFT OUTER JOIN the generated date series with transaction data so empty periods show as 0

amdp-hana-003.quickRevisionNotes:
What: HANA's procedural SQL extension used inside AMDP for multi-step database-side logic.

Used for:
- Multi-step aggregations and transformations with tabular variables
- Date series and time bucket generation with built-in functions
- Conditional transformations using CASE WHEN at set level

Key syntax / tables / Tcodes:
- DECLARE lv_var TYPE := value; :lv_var to reference
- lt_data = SELECT ...; :lt_data for tabular reference
- DAYS_BETWEEN, ADD_DAYS, COALESCE, CURRENT_DATE, SERIES_GENERATE_DATE
- OPTIONS READ-ONLY to enforce read-only contract

Interview keyword: set-based SQLScript inside AMDP, avoid LOOP
